creator: schilling@fokus.gmd.de
text:
>From ibex@emmi.physik.TU-Berlin.DE Sat Feb 24 12:06:59 2001

>On the other hand it would be really nice if mkisofs wouldn't use
>internal functions... 

No problem if there would be working standard functions on FreeBSD....

>BTW, Joerg, is there any specific reason why you are using ecvt()/fcvt()/
>gcvt() and providing a compatibilty "layer" for BSD instead of using
>standard functions?

May be you missunderstood things: ecvt()/fvgt()/gcvt() _are_ the standard
functions. 

I need them to write a portable printf().


A portable printf() helps a lot to port applications.
It is definitely needed in one or two years with the upcomming POSIX-2001/2
Otherwise it will be close to impossible to write programs that port to
non POSIX systems. This is because fundamental system types _then_ may be > long.


BTW: I found a bug in the way large files are handled in FreeBSD

FreeBSD has a off_t which is > long. Note that current POSIX as well as
UNIX-98 forbids any fundamental system type to be > long.

Having off_t > long by default is dangerous because it could cause conforming
applications to corrupt files. This is why 4-5 years ago there has been
a large file summit which defines how large files may be implemented 
without being dangerous.

A LFS conforming application must tell the compilation environment
that it conforms and that the environment may switch to off_t > long.


Please fix this Large file bug...

Jörg
